titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/280

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=501 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:56:25.405849+00 | translation moa=502 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:56:31.348363+00

p.span:
Vniuersis matris ecclesie filiis hoc scriptum uisuris et audituris Alexander de Chatthou salutem . Nouerit uniuersitas uestra quod cum terra de
Rasawes abbati et Monachis de Melros esset a uero domino per certas diuisas concessa et tradita perpetuo iure possidenda  ego Alexander estimans
me ius habere in quadam parte terre intra dictas diuisas contente eandem parte michi uendicaui . Cum uero mihi liquido constaret me nil iuris habere in predicta
parte terre  ego de consilio amicorum meorum renuntiaui inperpetuum pro me et heredibus meis omni actioni et omni iuri agendi siquid ego et
heredes mei habuimus uel habere potuimus uel habituri fuimus in predicta parte terre quacumque ratione . Ad obseruationem uero firme pacis inter me et prefatos
Monachos communi assensu nostro prouise sunt diuise iste inter terram meam et terram dictorum Monachorum . Scilicet ex orientali parte de Der
stret ascendendo de Calne per sicum usque in scolceuescluch et per eundem sicum ascendendo usque ad crucem de assensu nostro constructam
et sic in directum usque ad capud de seteburne et per eandem burnam descendendo usque ad Burnam que descendit de Thedbrichtes
hop et sic descendendo usque ad riuulum de Cuithenhop . Hee quidem diuise a me et Monachis prouise sunt et inuiolabi
liter concesse . Ita quod ego pro me et heredibus meis et successoribus meis uniuersis penitus et perpetuo renunciaui omni iuri et cla
mio nobis competentibus uel inperpetuum competituris ultra diuisas prenominatas . Actum anno gracie . Millesimo . ducentesimo . vicesimo sexto . Hiis testibus .
Willelmo filio iohannis . Bernardo de hauden . Gilberto de Wittun’ . Adam de Chattun’ . et Multis Aliis .

div.p: To all sons of Mother Church who shall view or hear this writing Alexander of Chatto sends greeting. Your community shall know that when the land of Rasawes had been granted to the abbot and monks of Melrose by the true lord by certain bounds and handed over to be possessed by everlasting right, I Alexander considering myself to have right in a portion of the land contained between the said boundaries claimed that same portion for myself. Since however it became clear to me that I have no right in the aforesaid portion of land, on the advice of my friends I have for ever renounced for myself and my heirs every action and every right to be pursued, if I and my heirs had or could have had or might have in future any at all in the aforesaid portion of the land for whatever reason. For the observance of firm peace between me and the aforesaid monks these boundaries have been established by our common agreement between my land and the land of the said monks. That is from the eastern side of 'Derestreet' going upwards from 'Calne' by the syke as far as 'Scolceuescluch' and by the same syke going upwards as far as the cross build with our agreement and thus straight on as far as the head of 'Seteburne' and by the same burn going downwards as far as the burn which goes down from 'Thedbrichteshop' and thus descending as far as the small stream of 'Cuithenhop'. These boundaries have been established and inviolably granted by me and the monks, so that I for myself and my heirs and all my successors have entirely renounced every right and claim competent to us now or for ever in the future over the boundaries mentioned beforehand. Enacted in the year of grace 1226. With these as witnesses: William son of John; Bernard of Howden; Gilbert of Whitton; Adam of Chatto; and many others.
